data.title: Sagimet Biosciences rockets 30% on pricing $175M equity offering
data.description:
• Clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company Sagimet Biosciences (SGMT) stock price jumped about 30% on Monday pre-market hours as the company priced an underwritten offering of ~29M shares at $6.00 per share.
  • The gross proceeds of $175M. Net proceeds combined with existing capital will be used to fund a Phase 3 trial for denifanstat in acne, advance TVB-3567 and a topical FASN inhibitor, and cover general corporate expenses. The company expects this funding to sustain its acne program operations and support the Phase 3 trial readout through 2028.
  • The offering is expected to close on or about April 28, 2026.

data.title: Applied Optoelectronics leads all large-cap IT stocks in YoY CapEx growth
data.description:
A rising capital expenditure figure often signals that a company is betting heavily on its own future growth. Below is a list of U.S. large-cap IT stocks ranked by their year-over-year CapEx growth percentage, spanning multiple sectors and market capitalizations.

The list spans multiple sectors within technology, including Communications Equipment, Semiconductors, Application Software, Systems Software, Electronic Manufacturing Services, and Semiconductor Materials & Equipment.

Applied Optoelectronics (AAOI) tops the list with a CapEx growth rate of 318.80% year-over-year, reflecting the company's rapid infrastructure expansion amid surging demand for high-speed optical networking components.

Arista Networks (ANET) and MACOM Technology Solutions (MTSI) follow in second and third place with growth rates of 273.44% and 241.13% respectively, while Strategy Inc (MSTR) and Oracle (ORCL) round out the top five at 235.45% and 223.11%.

Guidewire Software (GWRE) and FormFactor (FORM) occupy the sixth and seventh positions with CapEx growth of 206.37% and 169.69%, while Palantir Technologies (PLTR) follows at 168.18%. Palo Alto Networks (PANW) and Sanmina Corporation (SANM) round out the list at 130.88% and 130.73% respectively.

Notably, Sanmina (SANM) is the only stock on the list to carry a Buy Quant Rating of 4.07, a meaningful distinction in a group where most names hold Hold ratings, and Strategy Inc (MSTR) sits closest to bearish territory with a Hold rating of 2.59.

Here is the list:

  1. Applied Optoelectronics, Inc. (AAOI), CapEx growth YoY: 318.80%

  2. Arista Networks, Inc. (ANET), CapEx growth YoY: 273.44%

  3. MACOM Technology Solutions Holdings, Inc. (MTSI), CapEx growth YoY: 241.13%

  4. Strategy Inc (MSTR), CapEx growth YoY: 235.45%

  5. Oracle Corporation (ORCL), CapEx growth YoY: 223.11%

  6. Guidewire Software, Inc. (GWRE), CapEx growth YoY: 206.37%

  7. FormFactor, Inc. (FORM), CapEx growth YoY: 169.69%

  8. Palantir Technologies Inc. (PLTR), CapEx growth YoY: 168.18%

  9. Palo Alto Networks, Inc. (PANW), CapEx growth YoY: 130.88%

  10. Sanmina Corporation (SANM), CapEx growth YoY: 130.73%

Tech ETFs: (VGT), (XLK), (IYW), (FTEC), (IXN), and (RSPT)

data.title: Ligand to buy XOMA Royalty for $39/share
data.description:
Ligand Pharmaceuticals (LGND) has agreed to acquire XOMA Royalty (XOMA) for $39.00 per share of common stock in cash, for a total equity value of approximately $739M.

The transaction has been unanimously approved by the Ligand and XOMA Royalty boards of directors and is expected to close in the third quarter of 2026, subject to customary closing conditions.

The cash consideration for the transaction is expected to be funded with Ligand’s existing cash on hand and borrowings under its existing credit facility.

The transaction is expected to be immediately accretive to Ligand’s earnings per share. The company raised its 2026 revenue guidance to $270M-$310M from $245M-$285M and increased adjusted diluted EPS guidance to $8.50-$9.50 from $8.00-$9.00. Ligand also expects the deal to add $1.50 per share to adjusted EPS in 2027.

The transaction also adds more than 120 commercial, clinical, and preclinical assets to Ligand’s royalty portfolio. As a result, the company raised its royalty revenue outlook to $225M-$250M from $200M-$225M.

"The acquisition of XOMA Royalty presents a compelling opportunity for us to strengthen and diversify our portfolio across all stages of clinical development and accelerate our long-term profitable growth. This acquisition will add seven marketed products and nearly double our portfolio of Phase 2 and 3 assets, which we believe will create significant value for our stockholders, all through a single transaction,” said Todd Davis, CEO of Ligand.

Ligand will hold an investor call at 8:00 a.m. ET today to discuss today’s announcement. LGND and XOMA both rose 2.9% premarket.

data.title: Alibaba rolls out viral Happy Horse AI model in beta
data.description:
• Alibaba (BABA) said on Monday that it has rolled out the Happy Horse AI model, which went viral earlier this year, in beta.
  • “We’re excited to roll out HappyHorse 1.0 in limited beta,” Alibaba wrote on X. “It is now accessible to creators globally via the HappyHorse official website or through API service on Alibaba Cloud Model Studio.”
  • Shares of the Chinese tech giant fell in premarket trading.

data.title: Moody's changes China's outlook to stable from negative; affirms A1 rating
data.description:
Moody's Ratings affirmed the government of China's local and foreign-currency issuer and senior unsecured ratings at A1 and changed its outlook to stable from negative, the ratings firm said Monday.

"The stabilization of the outlook reflects our assessment that economic and fiscal strength will be resilient to ongoing domestic as well as trade and geopolitical challenges," Moody's said in a statement. It also said the outlook is supported by sustained growth and effective debt management, despite domestic and external challenges.

The ratings company expects the country's export growth to moderate, but its competitiveness and the resilience of its exports to rapid changes in the global trade picture will only slow GDP growth gradually in the medium term. At the same time, government policies that prioritize investment in high-productivity sectors while managing supply imbalances will improve capital efficiency, Moody's said.

Furthermore, China's policymakers are expected to manage the debt resolution process for regional and local governments in a controlled fashion while general government debt rises due to substantial policy support to the economy.

Moody's estimates China's real GDP growth at 4.5% in 2026 and 4.2% in 2027. Its debt is projected to rise to 82.4% in 2027 from 68.5% in 2025 and above 90% by the end of the decade.

The Chinese yuan rose 0.2% against the U.S. dollar in morning trading, New York time. The State Street SPDR China ETF (GXC) climbed 1.1%, the iShares MSCI China ETF (MCHI) edged down 0.2%, and the iShares China Large-Cap ETF (FXI) fell 0.8% in premarket trading.

data.title: HBT Financial Non-GAAP EPS of $0.68 beats by $0.06, revenue of $67.33M beats by $0.35M
data.description:
• HBT Financial press release (HBT): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.68 beats by $0.06.
  • Revenue of $67.33M (+16.1% Y/Y) beats by $0.35M.
  • Net interest margin increased 8 basis points to 4.20% and net interest margin (tax-equivalent basis) increased 9 basis points to 4.25%

data.title: CareTrust buys $628M in healthcare properties
data.description:
CareTrust REIT (CTRE) on Monday said it closed about $628M of investments in April, which includes the acquisition of 15 skilled nursing facilities in California.

The company said the California transaction totaled about $380M, including costs, and covered roughly 1700 beds.

The properties were leased back to affiliates of the seller under a long-term triple-net lease. CareTrust also originated two related loans totaling $163M secured by skilled nursing portfolios in California and Washington.

CareTrust acquired four care homes in the U.K. for about £42M and said it expects to buy a fifth property for about £9M after approvals. It also bought a 124-bed skilled nursing facility in Wyoming for about $20M.

The investments carry a blended stabilized yield of 8.8% and were funded using a combination of proceeds received from settled equity forward contracts and borrowings under its revolving credit facility, the company said.

Including the latest deals, CareTrust said its 2026 year-to-date investments total about $990M, with a near-term pipeline of about $450M.

Separately, Moody's Ratings assigned the company and its senior unsecured notes an investment-grade Baa3 rating with a stable outlook.

data.title: BASF hikes prices again for additives in plastic applications on Middle East war disruption
data.description:
BASF (BASFY) said Monday it is raising prices for products within its global portfolio of antioxidants, process stabilizers, and light stabilizers for plastic applications, citing substantial increases in raw material prices, energy, and logistic costs due to the Middle East war.

BASF (BASFY) said it will boost prices for the plastic-protecting chemicals, commonly used in the car and consumer goods industries, by as much as 25% effective immediately, in addition to a 20% hike announced in early March.

The products impacted are used to prolong the lifespan of plastics in various materials, including those used to make wire and cabling insulation, steering wheels, car door trims, and outdoor plastic furniture.
